page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 24 dedicated vCPUs and 96.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 24 physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core topology. The hardware allocation provides a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It operates on the KVM hypervisor, includes no local storage, and delivers an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

Benchmark data reveals a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, including stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, fall into the bottom 25%, indicating weaker single-threaded execution. This is further highlighted by poor single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds, which rank in the bottom 10%. Conversely, multi-core performance is more robust, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores all placing in the average middle 50% tier. Memory bandwidth tests also demonstrate average performance, though the Passmark memory score is positioned in the bottom 25%.

Given its balanced resource allocation, the ecs.g5.6xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads that can leverage all 24 dedicated cores, offsetting the lower single-core efficiency. It is well-suited for static web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded data processing, where it achieves average throughput. However, it is less optimal for single-threaded applications or workloads requiring high-speed local storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs and 96.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under the KVM hypervisor, it provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate a notable tradeoff: single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds are weak, ranking in the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. However, the instance compensates with average multi-core CPU performance, average memory bandwidth, and stable multi-threaded decompression capabilities. With an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance is qualitatively cost-effective for balanced, multi-threaded workloads. It is best suited for static web serving, database operations, and general-purpose applications that do not rely heavily on single-threaded speed or local storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps network speed, offering balanced multi-core performance for database and web workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 24 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring 24 dedicated cores and 96 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded workloads and web serving.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 96.0 GB of RAM
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Average multi-core CPU and database operation performance
Weaker single-core and single-threaded compression speeds
Suited for static web serving and multi-threaded workloads
